Quote: Reversed course on this, and bought the weekend house. The kids love it beyond belief, we're building memories as a family, and it's amazing to have a home away from home gataway. Is it a good investment? No, but who cares. It's one of the best things we ever did!
Author: @realEstateTrent

Quote: Test. A person adopts another’s Frame when he accepts the other view of the world. For example, if you become self-conscious or question yourself when your wife tells you ‘all you ever think about is sex’ you are accepting her view of reality in place of your view of reality. That is not a strong Frame and it is definitely not you at your best.
Author: Bluepill Professor

Quote: (While it is true that we generally live longer than people used to, or than people in some traditional cultures do, most of our added years owe to gains in infant mortality and child health, not diet.)
Author: Michael Pollan

Quote: “In the depths of winter, I finally learned that within me there lay an invincible summer,” said Albert Camus. We must only outlast the season.
Author: John Soforic

Quote: The Monk encourages us to consider how we spend our time, not our money. Marrying our values and passions to the energy we invest in work, it suggests, increases the significance of each moment.
Author: Randy Komisar with Kent Lineback

Quote: Freed from your usual duties, released from the constraints of your day-to-day identity, you find the mental space to do something a little bit different.
Author: Anne-Laure Le Cunff

Quote: Disposing of corpses is an important part of public hygiene, and many areas of India effectively relied on vultures to assist with that job. Once vultures started to decline, places where they were helping with corpse cleanup experienced an uptick in human mortality.
Author: @cremieuxrecueil

Quote: If we reframe the question of how we spend our time, however, we can start to figure out how long each of our tasks actually takes. Perhaps we need only four hours a day to get our work done.
Author: Paul Jarvis

Quote: In contrast, a disc subjected to high compressive loads that do not involve bending (i.e. good form with the powerlifts) will get stiffer and resilient to delamination. This is what the strength athlete needs. Most athletes cannot have it both ways – that being a mobile spine that bears high load. Strength athletes should avoid yoga, and spine stretching. Buttwink, when squatting, will eventually cause problems in most people. Stay tight, “locked in,” let the hips power the rigid torso.
Author: Stuart McGill and Brian Carroll

Quote: The “Modern Critical Essays” series, edited by Harold Bloom (1984: Modern Critical Essays; Anna Karenina: Modern Critical Essays, and so on) contains essays by a number of well-known critics and will give you a good overview of the critical take on a work.
Author: Susan Wise Bauer
Theme: Reading, Writing & the Life of the Mind

Quote: Hi Casey. I’m reading a book about how to bring my friends together and host a cocktail party. I want to throw my first one on Wednesday the 8th at 7 p.m. Would you come? Can I send you some more info?
Author: Nick Gray

Quote: After that, I decided to write down the three things I want for each of the people I love the most and then ask: Am I investing in those things in their lives? Am I putting my time, energy, affection, expertise, and money toward the development of these assets and qualities? Am I modeling them with my own behavior? Do I need a new investment strategy?
Author: Arthur C. Brooks
Theme: Purpose, Meaning & the Second Half of Life

Quote: I was too proud (aka insecure) to reach out to potential new friends to see if anyone wanted to hang out, for fear of rejection. And I know now that I’m not alone in feeling these things.
Author: Radha Agrawal

Quote: How could it be that “guys who know where the market is heading are no longer at the Board of Trade. They are either retired or broke. And I can’t think of any that are retired.” Classic Klipp.
Author: Mark Spitznagel

Quote: Some people, usually senior men, work at home one day a week; this is a particularly common method of time-consolidation for editors or research scientists.
Author: Peter F. Drucker

Quote: Catherine stood back up. “Bill Browder. He is CEO of the Hermitage Fund, which is the biggest investor in the Russian stock market. And I believe the prime minister of the United Kingdom might have discussed this with you today.”
Author: Bill Browder

Quote: His lack of an undergraduate degree threatened to derail him, but a family friend, former Harvard Law School Dean Roscoe Pound, interceded on Charlie's behalf. Charlie was admitted, despite the determination of the admissions office to first send him back to college.
Author: Charles T. Munger (edited by Peter D. Kaufman)

Quote: There was a common but shortsighted feeling that the first job out of school was a life-or-death decision. In reality, these first jobs are often all but irrelevant, given the direction that our careers later take.
Author: Guy Spier
Source: The Education of a Value Investor

Quote: YOU ARE NOT STARVING. Humans can survive thirty days without food. You can make it a few extra hours. You can actually make it a few days without any issue. I fast twenty-four hours fairly regularly. I do seventy-two-hour fasts approximately every three months.
Author: Jocko Willink

Quote: Act with fortitude and honor,” he wrote to a distraught friend in serious financial and legal trouble of the man’s own making. “If you cannot reasonably hope for a favorable extrication, do not plunge deeper. Have the courage to make a full stop.
Author: Ryan Holiday

Quote: There is hope. We know four of the most important sources of marital conflict in the transition to parenthood: sleep loss, social isolation, unequal workload, and depression. We will examine each. Couples who make themselves aware of these can become vigilant about their behavior, and they tend to do better. We also know that not every marriage follows this depressing course of events.
Author: John Medina

Quote: “Love the Lord your God…with all your mind” (Matthew 22:37).
Author: Ron Rhodes

Quote: His recommended reading materials (see Appendix) provide some guidance. Guns, Germs, and Steel, The selfish Gene, Ice Age, and Darwin's Blind Spot all have a certain theme: a focus on the aforementioned issue of "competitive destruction" and an examination of why some entities are nevertheless able to adapt, survive, and even dominate over time.
Author: Charles T. Munger (edited by Peter D. Kaufman)

Quote: Despite several millennia of such warnings, however, there is still an overwhelming social compulsion — an insanity of consensus, if you will — to get rich from life rather than live richly, to “do well” in the world instead of living well.
Author: Rolf Potts

Quote: Anyway so I think guys should roll up their sleeves. It’s a good look. It’s giving capable. It’s giving strong. It’s giving “I got this.” Plus I like seeing veins. And cool watches.
Author: @rachcorrine

Quote: In the decade since, he’s devoted a prolific research career toward studying the relationship between giving and success. Grant has found that people fall into one of three orientations: There are givers, who seek opportunities to give to others without expectation of anything in return; takers, who hoard resources and look for ways others can serve them; and finally matchers, who like to give as much as they get.
Author: Keith Ferrazzi

Quote: How can you focus exclusively on whether another child would make you happier? What about the child? Unless your baby is truly unlucky, he will almost certainly be happy to be alive.
Author: Bryan Caplan
Source: Selfish Reasons to Have More Kids

Quote: It’s pretty easy to tell when someone is, and isn’t, investing in inspiration. I’ve never met anyone yet who was living his or her passion—I mean “pedal to the metal”—who was not a heavy investor in inspiration.
Author: Zig Ziglar

Quote: Remember, in recession, it’s not just houses that are cheap. They’re giving cars away, and you can go to Best Buy and buy the whole store. Everything is on sale.
Author: Jared Dillian

Quote: 𝗖𝗼𝗹𝘂𝗺𝗯𝘂𝘀: Two strangers bond over architecture and family struggles, their quiet conversations healing wounds neither knew they had.
Author: @bluewmist

Quote: The major foci of the coffeehouse were coffee, camaraderie, and acoustic music. The music was mostly traditional folk and bluegrass and what we called “homemade music,” now commonly described as “contemporary folk.” For years, the Post was the only venue in New Orleans for local singer/songwriters of contemporary folk music. Weekly “open mic” nights were open to any musicians as an informal audition.
Author: Ray Oldenburg

Quote: Pogačar in press conference: "We witnessed one of the greatest performances on the climb ever. When I checked my numbers after it was really crazy, especially the part where Mateo Jorgenson and Jonas went on the front. Those were the highest numbers I ever did in my career."
Author: @CyclingGraphs

Quote: Major life cheat code: Learn to work without validation. Write when nobody’s reading. Build when nobody’s watching. Train when nobody’s cheering. It doesn’t take talent. It just takes courage. Those with the courage to work in the dark will eventually shine in the light.
Author: @SahilBloom

Quote: In his parable of Gyges in The Republic, a shepherd finds a ring, the turning of which on his finger makes him invisible. Because he is thus rendered invisible, he can rob or exploit his neighbors at will, since he is free from the risk of consequences. Would any of us act morally if we knew we were not to be held accountable?
Author: James Hollis
Source: Why Good People Do Bad Things

Quote: Without growth, meritocracy is just a nice word. How do you create opportunities for the most talented people if a company is not expanding? How do you remunerate your most successful employees if the firm’s earnings are not increasing?
Author: Cristiane Correa

Quote: We recommend capitalizing R&D expenses in return on invested capital, or ROIC, calculations, as they are core investments in the business. In addition, we don’t like to see prolonged cuts in this line item for cost-saving purposes.
Author: Heather Brilliant and Elizabeth Collins

Quote: I am extremely disappointed in this site for killing both DMs and threads. There has been a definite and extreme turn towards promoting slop, low-quality engagement, and moving effortful post-making over to different websites. Total own-goal.
Author: @cremieuxrecueil

Quote: The thing is, it's really hard to be roommates with people if your suitcases are much better than theirs--if yours are really good ones and theirs aren't. You think if they're intelligent and all, the other person, and have a good sense of humor, that they don't give a damn whose suitcases are better, but they do. They really do. It's one of the reasons why I roomed with a stupid bastard like Stradlater. At least his suitcases were as good as mine.
Author: J. D. Salinger

Quote: We may not be able to control the financial markets, but we can exert some control over our own bodies, what we eat, how often we go to the gym, who we spend time with, etc. By doing so we gain a toehold amidst the chaos, and this can convince us – delude us, if you will – into believing we are in charge.
Author: John Coates
Source: The Hour Between Dog and Wolf

Quote: The accounting mumbo jumbo stocks should lead a stockholder to conclude: If you own it and cannot understand it, sell it. If you do not own it and cannot understand it, consider selling it short if you think the obscurity is just the tip of the iceberg.
Author: Kathryn F. Staley

Quote: This is where it begins. In the darkness. Before the sun and the birds and the world. Every day. When the alarm sounds. IT IS TIME. Rise. Despite fatigue and soreness. Curse the warmth of the bed. Curse the comfort of the pillow. Fight the temptation of weakness. Get up and go. Do it quickly, without thought. Do not reason with weakness. You cannot. You must only take action. Get up and GO.
Author: Jocko Willink

Quote: The philosophy asserts that virtue (meaning, chiefly, the four cardinal virtues of self-control, courage, justice, and wisdom) is happiness, and it is our perceptions of things—rather than the things themselves—that cause most of our trouble.
Author: Ryan Holiday and Stephen Hanselman

Quote: Lorna Court, a block of apartments encapsulating all Johannesburg’s supposed potential to become the next Manhattan, now stands for many as a symbol of the City of Gold’s decline. It is now an uninhabited, uninhabitable symbol of dilapidated and condemned architecture...
Author: @tebogokhaas

Quote: It’s important here to interject the fact that we in those prisons were not an unsophisticated group. We were almost all fighter pilots, all had bachelor’s degrees, and more than half of us had at least one advanced degree.
Author: James B. Stockdale
Source: Thoughts of a Philosophical Fighter Pilot

Quote: Interest in the private self was on the upswing, impelled not only by Protestantism but by capitalism, which encouraged each person to think of himself (or herself) as an individual, able to rise up through society’s levels toward wealth and leisure. The self was no longer part of a rigid, unshifting feudal system, with responsibilities beginning at birth and never changing thereafter. The self was free.
Author: Susan Wise Bauer
Theme: Reading, Writing & the Life of the Mind

Quote: Hard to believe that someone who writes and speaks so frequently about morality and ethics would use sex as a weapon and sleep with all of his female co-authors. A real head scratcher.
Author: @robkhenderson

Quote: Instead of nitpicking everything that’s going “wrong,” you focus your awareness on all the things that are going right and feel grateful for those things.
Author: Radha Agrawal

Quote: The processes by which potential friends might find one another and by which friendships not suited to the home might be nurtured outside it are severely thwarted by the limited features and facilities of the modern suburb.
Author: Ray Oldenburg

Quote: Another of Uncle Henry’s nuggets of advice to the young came drifting back into his mind from years ago: There is nowhere else in the world where you can keep busy doing so little and enjoying it so much. One day you’ll understand.
Author: Peter Mayle

Quote: In his book The Start-Up of You, Reid suggests making at least one quality introduction a month. If you can make them valuable, I’d say double that!
Author: Keith Ferrazzi

Quote: I was so enraged by this elitist attitude that I finally owned up to my own past and wrote about my ten-thousand-dollar college experience in The New York Times—about how my education was perfectly good, and how it gave me the opportunity to build my life and career.
Author: Arthur C. Brooks
Theme: Purpose, Meaning & the Second Half of Life

Quote: Avoid compulsive whiners, chronic pessimists, mindless bleeding hearts, and self-conscious hipsters, since these kinds of people (who are surprisingly common along the travel trail) have a way of turning travel into a tiresome farce.
Author: Rolf Potts

Quote: If I could only give you one piece of career advice, it would be to always give more than is expected of you. When you do that, you instantly stand out as a person of character...There is no simpler formula for social and career success.
Author: @robkhenderson

Quote: We enhance most experiences in these two ways-by increasing the directness of our involvement in an activity and by increasing our social involvement.
Author: Ray Oldenburg

Quote: But here’s the thing: you already have a terminal diagnosis. We all do! As the writer Edmund Wilson put it, “Death is one prophecy that never fails.” Every person is born with a death sentence. Each second that passes by is one you’ll never get back.
Author: Ryan Holiday and Stephen Hanselman

Quote: Before I use these techniques or send any class out to practice these techniques, I remind myself and them of one everlasting rule that will dramatically increase your probability of success; it is all about them.
Author: Robin Dreeke

Quote: There is a pretty unanimous verdict that it is health promoting and the body composition research isn’t necessarily about finding out if it is ‘best’, as much as it is finding out if it is useful and why it might be useful.
Author: Ben Carpenter

Quote: It’s easy to understand how a man who spends the best part of his busy days at the pressure-packed juncture of life and death could become absorbed in philosophical thought.
Author: James B. Stockdale
Source: Thoughts of a Philosophical Fighter Pilot

Quote: Clearly, the quality of a decision cannot be solely judged based on its outcome, but such a point seems to be voiced only by people who fail (those who succeed attribute their success to the quality of their decision).
Author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb

Quote: A HEALTHY PERSON (v2) These are some of the most important factors to being a "healthy" person (or in other words, someone who reduces their mortality risk) Input from many peers for v2 here!
Author: @drpetertierney

Quote: Like Cope, however, I have embraced a broader conception of work and agree with him that the search for work worth doing is the real work and one of the most important pursuits in life.
Author: Paul Millerd

Quote: Self-doubt is that part of the soul that is able to taste the bitter in life as well as the sweet. It is open to a side of life that a sunny disposition must ignore in order to carry on smiling.
Author: David Whyte

Quote: Yet the same Robert C. Merton later introduced himself as the “founding partner” of a hedge fund that aimed at taking advantage of market inefficiencies. Setting aside the fact that Merton’s hedge fund blew up rather spectacularly from the black swan problem (with characteristic denial), his “founding” such a hedge fund requires, by implication, that he agrees with Shiller about the inefficiency of the market.
Author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb

Quote: A basic industry with few players, rational management, barriers to entry, a lack of exit barriers and non-complex rules of engagement is the perfect setting for companies to engage in cooperative behaviour.
Author: Edward Chancellor (editor)

Quote: I used to be afraid of failing at something that really mattered to me, but now I’m more afraid of succeeding at things that don’t matter.
Author: Bob Goff

Quote: Even the samurai oath is oriented toward action: I will never fall behind others in pursuing the way of the warrior. I will always be ready to serve my lord. I will honor my parents. I will serve compassionately for the benefit of others. Hagakure, the most famous collection of samurai wisdom, instructs: “The extent of one’s courage or cowardice cannot be measured in ordinary times. All is revealed when something happens.”
Author: Ben Horowitz
Source: What You Do Is Who You Are

Quote: Be persistent, but never pushy. Masculine initiation should say "I'm coming to get you, girl!" If she needs time, respect it. Anticipation is your best friend. Let her know you’re looking forward to it when the time is right, and watch how the dynamic changes.
Author: @georgehayworth

Quote: The Vocabulary from Classical Roots series, also from Educators Publishing Service, is a good follow-up to Wordly Wise. As a matter of fact, many readers (not just those doing remedial work) may find this series helpful in preparing to read classic literature. Each lesson gives several Greek or Latin roots, lists of familiar words using those roots, and lists of unfamiliar words along with exercises in proper use.
Author: Susan Wise Bauer
Theme: Reading, Writing & the Life of the Mind

Quote: An efficient home-delivery media system, in contrast, tends to make shut-ins of otherwise healthy individuals; the more people receive news in isolation, the more they become susceptible to manipulation by those who control the media.
Author: Ray Oldenburg

Quote: Montessori gave us the greatest educational philosophy ever created. A deep knowledge curriculum pushed as young as possible. A maniacal focus on sustained concentration and joy in work. A radical pro-civilization and pro-human bent. And a fundamental goal of fostering agency.
Author: @mbateman

Quote: You also don’t need to quit your job to conduct your experiment full time. Albert Einstein conducted most of his research while being employed as a patent examiner. Haruki Murakami wrote his first two books while running a jazz club.
Author: Anne-Laure Le Cunff

Quote: It took several years and many smaller trips to decide that sailing for more than a year was something they definitely wanted to do.
Author: Paul Millerd

Quote: Dealing a severe blow to the company, the agency had declared its nanotainer an uncleared medical device and forbidden it from continuing to use it, he said.
Author: John Carreyrou

Quote: The first thirteen years of my conscious life, school was a prison-like environment. You must go there every day; you cannot leave. You have no control over your environment. You cannot avoid other kids in school, and I was simply not part of the social network.
Author: Eric Jorgenson

Quote: As Pascal observed three hundred years ago, “When we read too fast or too slowly, we understand nothing.”
Author: Mortimer J. Adler and Charles Van Doren
Theme: Reading, Writing & the Life of the Mind

Quote: “I am an artist, a painter. One question I ask my students is, ‘What is beauty?’They struggle with a definition and each one is specifically unique, but a consensus emerges of a feeling inside, a good, wholesome, intangible, transcendent feeling. It is a feeling of the moment, of here, now, and of place.
Author: Ray Oldenburg

Quote: Whereas we are used to keeping our social blinders on in modern retail situations, the atmosphere of a third place, both physical and social, fosters a convivial openness. This openness, however, should not be confused with a contact or therapy group, since people are not required to be social, only invited to be so.
Author: Ray Oldenburg

Quote: Instead, he found himself dispensing wisdom more than knowledge—advice about life and leadership. “You are our modern elder,” they told him. At first, he took umbrage at this title.
Author: Arthur C. Brooks
Theme: Purpose, Meaning & the Second Half of Life

Quote: Eleazar refused to be ensnared any longer. Everyone else ran from the enemy, but he put his foot down and said, “I’m not running. I will fight until I drop dead or until I drop to the field in victorious exhaustion. This is my moment, live or die.”
Author: Stephen Arterburn, Fred Stoeker and Mike Yorkey

Quote: While it was a job, and a job I took seriously, running the restaurant also afforded me great pleasure and it was a privilege to meet the people I did without losing the professionalism it was so important to maintain.
Author: Antonio Carluccio

Quote: Heraclitus, a philosopher born in the Persian Empire back in the fifth century BC, had it right when he wrote about men on the battlefield. “Out of every one hundred men,” he wrote, “ten shouldn’t even be there, eighty are just targets, nine are the real fighters, and we are lucky to have them, for they make the battle. Ah, but the one, one is a warrior…”
Author: David Goggins

Quote: Professors Richard Tedeschi and Lawrence Calhoun have suggested that many people who face crises often experience “post-traumatic growth” and that this manifests as an “appreciation for life in general, more meaningful interpersonal relationships, an increased sense of personal strength, changed priorities, and a richer existential and spiritual life.”
Author: Paul Millerd

Quote: Leave very wide margins (two to three inches) on either side of your paragraphs. c. When you have done this for the entire chapter, glance back over your summary paragraphs. Now write down your reactions to the information in each summary. Use the margins of your paper for this (a different pen color is also helpful).
Author: Susan Wise Bauer
Theme: Reading, Writing & the Life of the Mind

Quote: Your flaws and your strengths are two poles of the same traits. For instance, there is only a tiny difference between stubbornness and perseverance or between courage and foolishness. The sole difference is in the goal. It’s stupid stubbornness and reckless foolishness if the goal does not matter, and relentless perseverance and courage if it does. To earn dignity with your flaws own up to them, and make sure you push on things that matter.
Author: Kevin Kelly

Quote: Sean: The change that I have made is just being less passive. You do have a lot of autonomy here, and you do your own thing. The bad thing about it is you don’t force yourself to change as much. You want to be out of your comfort zone and put yourself on the line and make a bet and then get rewarded. I have had to move from being more passive to being more aggressive.
Author: Ari Kiev

Quote: It was said that science alone has a rigorous, empirical method that results in proof, not merely speculation or feeling. Also in the modern worldview, absolutely everything has a natural and therefore physical cause.
Author: Timothy Keller

Quote: According to Cicero, the best a young person can hope for is to get old before he dies, and it is not fitting to complain about getting the best outcome you could have ever reasonably wanted.
Author: Charles T. Munger (edited by Peter D. Kaufman)

Quote: Scrolling on the phone whether it be fakebook, reddit, or twitter. If you aren’t hopping on, getting the information you need, then hopping off to pursue actual goals, then you’re wasting time.
Author: Hunter Drew

Quote: Virginia Woolf moaned that the novelist was a “slave” to the necessity of selling books; she longed for a fiction that could be free, with “no plot, no comedy, no tragedy, no love interest, or catastrophe in the accepted style.”
Author: Susan Wise Bauer
Theme: Reading, Writing & the Life of the Mind

Quote: This brings us to the final room of the machine, a collection of what Dewane calls “deep work chambers” (he adopted the term “deep work” from my articles on the topic). Each chamber is conceived to be six by ten feet and protected by thick soundproof walls (Dewane’s plans call for eighteen inches of insulation). “The purpose of the deep work chamber is to allow for total focus and uninterrupted work flow,”
Author: Cal Newport

Quote: Older moms are more likely to conceive twins (even if they’ve conceived naturally), thanks to an age-related predisposition to releasing more than one egg at a time.
Author: Heidi Murkoff
Source: What to Expect When You're Expecting

Quote: This is why the true measure of time wealth isn’t how much free time you have, but how much of that you are spending in activities that you find valuable. You can spend that time with friends and family, on your mental and physical health, or accomplishing something in your career. Whatever you decide to do, be intentional with your time. Because it’s the only kind of wealth that you will never get more of.
Author: Nick Maggiulli

Quote: Apparently even tribal societies recognise depression. Depressed members are allowed to be weird, wander off and not do their usual duties for months to years. It is considered a relatively normal thing to happen to a person. I found this digging around the UK mind website.
Author: @frideswyth

Quote: Instead, the following little reminder sums up the three most essential parts of Stoic philosophy worth carrying with you every day, into every decision: Control your perceptions. Direct your actions properly. Willingly accept what’s outside your control. That’s all we need to do.
Author: Ryan Holiday and Stephen Hanselman

Quote: I suspect much of what you currently read regularly (the material about which you think “but I have to read that”) is about adding facts rather than increasing understanding.
Author: Robert G. Hagstrom

Quote: One of the best pieces of advice I’ve received: Put good things between you and the earth. Always invest in a good bed, shoes, and tires. Old school—but so true.
Author: @SahilBloom

Quote: Neutral feet Flat shoes A supple thoracic spine An efficient squatting technique Hip flexion Hip extension Ankle range of motion Warming up and cooling down Compression No hotspots Hydration Jumping and landing
Author: Kelly Starrett and T.J. Murphy

Quote: let Bacon’s distinction add relevance to my concentration on adversity on this graduation day of joy: “Adversity doth best induce virtue . . . while luxury doth best induce vice.”
Author: James B. Stockdale
Source: Thoughts of a Philosophical Fighter Pilot

Quote: Sometimes your best strategy is counter-intuitive. If you have a high paying job, but realize that charitable giving is what matters most to you, then the best strategy is not to quit your job to go hang mosquito nets in Africa, but actually to keep your job and make as much money as you can, while funding organizations in Africa that hang thousands of mosquito nets. (Unless your goal is just to look charitable. Then admit that to yourself, too.)
Author: Derek Sivers

Quote: How can we successfully create the best circumstance to pursue our passions, while we live a happy, fulfilling life, continuously growing, learning, and improving? We will tap into our biggest secret advantage—a healthy distance—and learn how to become remote in three ways: a) physically remote, aka worldbound (away from the desk), learning about investment ideas from all countries; b) intellectually remote, aka original thinking (away from the groupthink), buying at the right time; c) emotionally remote, aka mindfulness (away from the noise of price volatility) -- and being patient and disciplined.
Author: Bogumil K. Baranowski

Quote: As I said at the U.S.C. Business School, what your need is a latticework of mental models in your head. And you hang your actual experience and your vicarious experience (that you get from reading and so forth) on this latticework of powerful models. And, with that system, things gradually get to fit together in a way that enhances cognition.
Author: Charles T. Munger (edited by Peter D. Kaufman)
Theme: Reading, Writing & the Life of the Mind

Quote: Jesus Christ this was an incredible read I’ve been burnt out by my feed being flooded with *too many* X articles lately. Note: not everyone should write long-form on this short-form app Will however is posting back to back/must-read bangers (I think this is now my favorite)
Author: @brexton

Quote: Well, as a newly minted walking grifter, you know I’d be down! 😆 They are both of course good for sheer stress, and you are right about the order of magnitude. However, it appears that chronic pulses appear to be more effective for this purpose. You can see them directly compared in this study. Also this second paper on the Tsimane is great, where you can see the effects at a population level. The authors note they believe it’s due to chronic lower level movement.
Author: @gregmushen
Theme: Purpose, Meaning & the Second Half of Life

Quote: In philosophical conversations, follow his example—stay mostly silent; ask questions and listen intently.
Author: Epictetus (rendered by Sam Torode / Ancient Renewal)

Quote: People sin in their vocations, and they sin against their vocations. And in not being aware of what their vocations are—and that there is a spiritual dimension to work, family, and involvement in society—they are plagued by a lack of purpose, confused as to what they should do and how they should live and who they are.
Author: Gene Edward Veith Jr.

Quote: Don’t let your brain fool you into thinking that the risk is greater than the meaning you are missing. And are you worried that a new job will be worse than the old one? You needn’t be: Studies show that changing jobs usually leads to a greater sense of purpose and well-being.
Author: Arthur C. Brooks

Quote: It assumes that each gamble is independent of your past performance, but in real life, your ability to bet is contingent on the success of bets you have made in the past.
Author: Rory Sutherland

Quote: It is good that the old should resist the young, and that the young should prod the old; out of this tension, as out of the strife of the sexes and the classes, comes a creative tensile strength, a stimulated development, a secret and basic unity and movement of the whole.
Author: Will Durant and Ariel Durant

Quote: Blessed with riches and possibilities far beyond anything imagined by ancestors who tilled the unpredictable soil of medieval Europe, modern populations have nonetheless shown a remarkable capacity to feel that neither who they are nor what they have is quite enough.
Author: Alain de Botton

Quote: NASDAQ is a quote-driven market. The London Stock Exchange has both an order-driven and quote-driven system – its more liquid shares are traded on its order-driven system.
Author: Not found

Quote: No one has had so much divine favor That they could guarantee themselves tomorrow. God keeps our lives hurtling on, Spinning in a whirlwind.”
Author: Ryan Holiday and Stephen Hanselman

Quote: I also have the same hobby as him. Cillian Murphy expresses a love for simple home life and the quiet joys of solitude, stating, “I love being at home. My life is very simple. I read a lot of books. Watch a lot of movies. Listen to a lot of music. Walk the dog. Cook with the family. I am boring”. He values the peace and personal contentment found in his own company, seeing it as a luxurious and fulfilling experience rather than loneliness.
Author: @AlexTran677026
Theme: Reading, Writing & the Life of the Mind

Quote: When writing docs to be shared, use formatting to draw the reader to the most important ideas (no one will read your docs, they’ll skim it). Use color, bold, monospace for code, tables, diagrams.
Author: @GrahamHelton3
Theme: Reading, Writing & the Life of the Mind

Quote: Unreasonable expectations timewise, resulting in unnecessary frustration, due to a perceived feeling of failure. Achieving the extraordinary is not a linear process. The secret is to show up, do the work, and go home.
Author: Tim Ferriss

Quote: Build a Moat: Create a distinctive, defendable brand proposition. Getting credit for it from the customer (at full price) is the measure of its strength and essential to sustainable success.
Author: Jim Weber
Theme: Purpose, Meaning & the Second Half of Life

Quote: For it were better to die of hunger, exempt from grief and fear, than to live in affluence with perturbation; and it is better that your servant should be bad than you unhappy.
Author: Epictetus

Quote: For a rapidly expanding community, it’s critical that prospective members are welcome to participate in community behavior before adopting common values.
Author: Charles H. Vogl

Quote: Look at your daily schedule, on paper or online, and mark it up to identify four experiences: starting and stopping moments, uninterrupted work time, social time, and physical movement time.
Author: Bill Burnett and Dave Evans

Quote: An effective monologue occurs when someone (loudly or softly) revokes the ability to be interrupted. It is a masculine, conversational gesture and, as such, it feels especially strong when the feminine form claims it. Nicole Mary Kidman.
Author: @kyalbr

Quote: “It’s giving rich.” Summer loading. White on white (or creams!!) Think monochrome. Tonal. Brown belt. Brown loafers (come ON). A watch that’s not a Garmin (save for your runs). Sunglasses. (A pair that actually frames your face nicely - you might need to try them on.)
Author: @rachcorrine

Quote: The standard antidote to one's overactive hostility is to train oneself to defer reaction. As my smart friend Tom Murphy so frequently says, "You can always tell the man off tomorrow if it is such a good idea."
Author: Charles T. Munger (edited by Peter D. Kaufman)

Quote: You envy those who walk away from their jobs to spend more time with their families and the ministry work they’ve been dreaming about.
Author: Bob Buford

Quote: To focus on technique is like cramming your way through school. You sometimes get by, perhaps even get good grades, but if you don't pay the price day in and day out, you never achieve true mastery of the subjects you study or develop an educated mind. Did you ever consider how ridiculous it would be to try to cram on a farm --to forget to plant in the spring, play all summer and then cram in the fall to bring in the harvest? The farm is a natural system.
Author: Stephen R. Covey
Source: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People

Quote: Principle of Population in which Malthus wrote: "Human population grows exponentially, like compound interest in a bank account, but farm output rises at a slower, arithmetic rate; the result, human population will inevitably and repeatedly outstrip its food supply." Malthus noted that population can always outstrip resources but are held in check by diseases, war, predators, and by limited resources like food.
Author: Peter Bevelin

Quote: He made a good-quality decision that got a bad result. Pete Carroll was a victim of our tendency to equate the quality of a decision with the quality of its outcome. Poker players have a word for this: “resulting.”
Author: Annie Duke

Quote: Even though Telles is no longer involved in the daily running of the company, he still takes part in the final selection of the trainee program and tells those chosen that they can send him messages directly should they feel it necessary.
Author: Cristiane Correa

Quote: There really is no other option besides learning for someone who wants to change their circumstances. If you can’t learn effectively, then you’re never going to move forward.
Author: Peter Hollins
Source: The Science of Rapid Skill Acquisition

Quote: I used the famous “real-life Limitless pill,” modafinil, to turn my brain back on so I could finish my MBA at Wharton while working full-time.
Author: Dave Asprey

Quote: Imagine that in five years, you doubled your income. What could have been the most likely reason, amongst those in your control? Is there anything you can do today to make that happen?
Author: Luca Dellanna (foreword by Guy Spier)

Quote: We are all faced with this same challenge in the pursuit of our own goals: Will we work hard for something that can be taken away from us? Will we invest time and energy even if an outcome is not guaranteed? With the right motives we’re willing to proceed. With ego, we’re not.
Author: Ryan Holiday

Quote: He was not the corrupt publisher of a popular empire. He was an aristocrat aboard a yacht. He looked, she thought, like what one believes aristocracy to be when one is young: a brilliant kind of gaiety without guilt.
Author: Ayn Rand

Quote: We are responsible human beings and responsibility means “vouching for ourselves” and “standing behind everything we do” before whatever is behind life.
Author: Os Guinness

Quote: Others announce that all their wealth will go to charity. What is left unsaid is that they intend to spend the rest of their days on this earth living lavishly. They will not release their riches; death will release their riches for them.
Author: Johann Kurtz

Quote: To savor what I neglected in the busy time of building a family and career. To enjoy the gift of this life, the magic of being alive, the wonders of creation, the beauty and tenderness of other human beings. To serve and contribute to healing and more love in the world.
Author: Mark Hyman

Quote: I thought like this when younger but realize it's an extraordinary mistake. A sophomoric view of many hours of existence. You have a duty to be cheerful and charming wherever you are. You should act so that regardless of circumstance people are delighted when you arrive.
Author: @simonsarris
Theme: Reading, Writing & the Life of the Mind

Quote: You think you know fire causes heat, says Hume, but you do not know that, because causation is not something perceived.
Author: Arthur Schopenhauer

Quote: The thought of the trip refreshed me. So much so, in fact, that I set for myself one more goal: to be well enough to attend the next KPMG partners’ meeting, scheduled for early November—a couple of months past the time I was supposed to still be here. I intended to be there.
Author: "Eugene O'Kelly"

Quote: Become a world-class collector of emotional wounds and convert them into rocket fuel for greatness.
Author: @Kpaxs

Quote: Don’t pay your children for every little thing. But when you want to give your kids a major project or a recurring chore, make it worth their while. Trading favors works well, especially for younger children. “If you eat your dinner without complaining, you get dessert,” “You can watch TV after you clean up your toys,” and “I’ll give you a ride to the mall if you put the dishes away” are all good options. At the same time, there’s absolutely nothing wrong with paying cash. You’re not trying to raise a communist.
Author: Bryan Caplan
Source: Selfish Reasons to Have More Kids

Quote: A very different life from the urban corporate work world, but essentially vulnerable to the same belief that God created the world simply in order to further our careers.
Author: David Whyte

Quote: Our location, immediately adjacent to a major university, has encouraged us to offer a place that sharply contrasts with the abundance of smoke-filled, alcohol driven, casual companion watering holes and gaming establishments that surround us. Ours is a uniquely created and purposeful great good place for community and spiritual renewal.
Author: Ray Oldenburg

Quote: If holding Joyce’s hand in a shopping center makes her feel loved and cherished, I would be a fool to refuse to do it. I appreciate her coaching on how to show affection. I promised to care for her when I married her, and I meant every word of it. If she explains how I can best give her the care she wants, I’m willing to learn because I want her to be happy.
Author: Willard F. Harley Jr.

Quote: Don’t believe anything anyone says. Listen if you want, but always decide for yourself. Never agree with anything the same day you hear it, because some ideas are persuasively hypnotic. Wait a few days to decide what you really think. Don’t let ideas into your head or heart without your permission.
Author: Derek Sivers

Quote: Underachievement at work is not, however, simply about staying employed. It is also about keeping the other relationships in your life healthy. For example, if you’re going to spend weekends at work all the time, you’d better be making wheelbarrows full of money to compensate for all the time you’re not spending with your friends and family. Face the facts: you’re probably not going to be a millionaire, so why kill yourself? Go home and spend a lazy weekend with your kids.
Author: Ray Bennett

Quote: Deathbed Regret: Not spending enough time with my kids during the “magic years.” There’s a devastatingly short window of time during which you are your child’s entire world. After it, they have friends/partners that fill that role. I refuse to miss those precious years.
Author: @SahilBloom

Quote: So we avoid, repress, split off, project onto others, and rationalize. These are our elemental, primitive defenses against what seems to threaten our insecure or immature ego. Growing as a moral and psychological being obliges each of us to learn more about our Shadow and take it on in a continuing effort of consciousness and courage.
Author: James Hollis
Source: Why Good People Do Bad Things

Quote: When child mortality declined and education (especially for women) and development increased, birth rates decreased. Humanity will need more innovation as absolute world population continues to grow, but the growth rate is declining, rapidly.
Author: David Epstein

Quote: The harsh truth is, I can’t pick winning individual stocks and you can’t either. Nor can the vast majority who claim they can. It is extraordinarily difficult, expensive and a fool’s errand. Having the humility to accept this will do wonders for your ability to accumulate wealth.
Author: JL Collins

Quote: The advantage of a ridiculously ambitious goal is that it sets the bar very high so even if your effort falls short it may exceed an ordinary success.
Author: Kevin Kelly

Quote: Integrity is the ability to stand by an idea. That presupposes the ability to think. Thinking is something one doesn’t borrow or pawn.
Author: Ayn Rand

Quote: This ancient Sufi story was told to teach a simple lesson but one that we often ignore: The behavior of a system cannot be known just by knowing the elements of which the system is made.
Author: Donella H. Meadows (edited by Diana Wright)

Quote: Gold from Kravis, but begs the question, does youth hold the same value to someone whose goals have gone unmet? To someone whose purpose still sits on the other side of tomorrow?
Author: @piktoggle_
Theme: Purpose, Meaning & the Second Half of Life

Quote: Turn your focus inward, on yourself, and take the opportunity not to rest … But to make yourself better, faster, smarter, stronger. Because with those goals, nothing is ever finished.
Author: Jocko Willink

Quote: What urban life increasingly fails to provide, and what is so much missed, is convenient and open-ended socializing places where individuals can go without aim or arrangement and be greeted by people who know them and know how to enjoy a little time off.
Author: Ray Oldenburg

Quote: What tormented Ivan Ilyich most,” Tolstoy writes, “was the deception, the lie, which for some reason they all accepted, that he was not dying but was simply ill, and he only need keep quiet and undergo a treatment and then something very good would result.
Author: Atul Gawande

Quote: Creatine is not recommended for most endurance athletes both because of its mild anabolic effect and because it causes water retention, increasing the weight an athlete has to carry around when racing.
Author: Dr. Alex Harrison, Michelle Howe, and Dr. Melissa Davis

Quote: “Poets,” who register below-average positive and above-average negative, have trouble enjoying good things, and always know when there is a threat lurking.
Author: Arthur C. Brooks and Oprah Winfrey
Theme: Purpose, Meaning & the Second Half of Life

Quote: You’re freed from the worries of “impostor syndrome”—that anxiety of not being the expert you’re cracked up to be—because no one actually expects you to be any good. You’re liberated from expectation, from the weight of the past. In Zen Buddhism, this state is referred to as beginner’s mind. Your mind is ready for anything, open to everything. “In the beginner’s mind there are many possibilities,” writes Shunryu Suzuki. “In the expert’s mind there are few.”
Author: Tom Vanderbilt

Quote: Too often, convinced of our own intelligence, we stay in a comfort zone that ensures that we never feel stupid (and are never challenged to learn or reconsider what we know).
Author: Ryan Holiday

Quote: Interval training [often at midday or lunch break] and meditation together are beautiful habits to develop to cultivate the art of turning it on and turning it off.
Author: Tim Ferriss

Quote: Indeed, historians long ago observed that in the death camps of World War II, the unit of survival was the pair, not the solitary individual. It’s long been known, too, that married men and women generally live longer than do their single peers.
Author: Sue Johnson

Quote: Those things happening to him, in those offices of strangers, were only a kind of sub-reality, unsubstantial incidents in the path of a substance they could not reach or touch.
Author: Ayn Rand

Quote: The clock is running. What once looked like an eternity ahead of you is now within reach. And while you do not fear the end of the game, you do want to make sure that you finish well, that you leave something behind that no one can take away from you. If the first half was a quest for success, the second half is a journey to significance. The game is won or lost in the second half, not the first.
Author: Bob Buford

Quote: Reputation is invaluable. Freedom and independence are invaluable. Family and friends are invaluable. Being loved by those who you want to love you is invaluable. Happiness is invaluable. And your best shot at keeping these things is knowing when it’s time to stop taking risks that might harm them. Knowing when you have enough.
Author: Morgan Housel

Quote: A good position allows you to think clearly rather than be forced by circumstances into a decision. One reason the best in the world make consistently good decisions is they rarely find themselves forced into a decision by circumstances. You don’t need to be smarter than others to outperform them if you can out-position them. Anyone looks like a genius when they’re in a good position, and even the smartest person looks like an idiot when they’re in a bad one.
Author: Shane Parrish

Quote: This habit of committing far more time to learning and thinking than to doing is no accident.
Author: Charles T. Munger (edited by Peter D. Kaufman)

Quote: You cant live on just groovy emotions alone. You have to work with the underlying form of the universe too, the laws of nature which, when understood, can make work easier, sickness rarer and famine almost absent.
Author: Robert M. Pirsig
Source: Zen and the Art of Motorcycle Maintenance

Quote: Your goal is sexual purity. Here’s a good working definition of it, good because of its simplicity: you are sexually pure when you’re getting no sexual gratification from anyone or anything but your wife.
Author: Stephen Arterburn, Fred Stoeker and Mike Yorkey

Quote: "You're going to see your parents fifteen more times before they die." The opening to @SahilBloom's new book hits you in a way that won't let you look away.
Author: @zachpogrob

Quote: There is an appearance of humility in the protestation that the truth is much greater than any one of us can grasp, but if this is used to invalidate all claims to discern the truth it is in fact an arrogant claim to a kind of knowledge which is superior to [all others]…We have to ask: “What is the [absolute] vantage ground from which you claim to be able to relativize all the absolute claims these different scriptures make?”
Author: Timothy Keller

Quote: The market is the most powerful wealth-building tool of all time. The market always goes up and it is always a wild and rocky ride along the way.
Author: JL Collins

Quote: On the other hand, the reciprocity-based, religion-boosting idea of obtaining help from God in reciprocation for good human behavior has probably been vastly constructive.
Author: Charles T. Munger (edited by Peter D. Kaufman)

Quote: It just seems implausible this is what we are made of, essentially, nanotechnology about a billion years beyond anything we can design or make ourselves.
Author: @Andercot

Quote: In the case of most religions, I think it is. Christians in particular will find that Stoic doctrines resonate with their religious views. They will, for example, share the Stoics’ desire to attain tranquility, although Christians might call it peace. They will appreciate Marcus Aurelius’s injunction to “love mankind.”
Author: William B. Irvine
Source: A Guide to the Good Life

Quote: STAY WEIRD like karpathy said on dwarkesh podcast: “Humans collapse during the course of their lives … we end up saying more and more of the same stuff, and the learning rates go down” and henrik karlsson on writing: “It is what is odd about you that is interesting. If you work hard, you will be able to write like everyone else in your genre, but the result will never be rich as the texture of your own personality.”
Author: @masonwang025
Theme: Reading, Writing & the Life of the Mind

Quote: The habit of listening to single songs on repeat for focus
Author: Tim Ferriss

Quote: Her female mind is turning at a hundred miles an hour as she realizes that this is a formidable man who is not fazed by her. This man, she begins to quickly realize, is confident, and it is obvious that if he can treat her dismissively then he must be somebody who is wanted by other women. In the words of author Rollo Tomassi, this is a man who “gets it.” This dude is “The Prize.”
Author: Bluepill Professor

Quote: Here’s the other part: once you win, everyone is gunning for you. It’s during your moment at the top that you can afford ego the least—because the stakes are so much higher, the margins for error are so much smaller. If anything, your ability to listen, to hear feedback, to improve and grow matter more now than ever before.
Author: Ryan Holiday

Quote: When we act like others, we perceive ourselves as being more similar to them, and as a result we like them more. This is why dancing, music, chanting and moving in tandem are such common parts of collective rituals.
Author: Dimitris Xygalatas

Quote: In my experience, the same applies to mathematics. Being competent in math means both intelligent & grounded. Being a professional mathematician necessarily means disconnected from reality.
Author: @nntaleb

Quote: Amazing author lunch & dinner today with: -AJ Jacobs (“The Year of Living Biblically”) -Bree Groff (“Today Was Fun”) -Yeonmi Park (“In Order to Live”) -Scott Barry Kaufman (“Transcend”) -Rob Henderson (“Troubled”) -Jon Goodman (“Unhinged Habits”)
Author: @AlexAndBooks_

Quote: Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn, one of the moral titans of our time, decided to write his nation’s hidden history and was reviled, imprisoned and deported for his pains.
Author: Christopher Hitchens

Quote: It wasn’t physically impossible, it wasn’t biologically absurd, to imagine life without money.
Author: Greg Egan

Quote: Complimentary lifestyle factors - particularly getting adequate sleep to regulate appetite hormones - contribute another five percent.
Author: Mark Sisson
Source: The Primal Blueprint 21-Day Total Body Transformation

Quote: Moreover, the struggle against the weaknesses in yourself is never a solitary struggle. No person can achieve self-mastery on his or her own. Individual will, reason, compassion, and character are not strong enough to consistently defeat selfishness, pride, greed, and self-deception. Everybody needs redemptive assistance from outside—from family, friends, ancestors, rules, traditions, institutions, exemplars, and, for believers, God. We all need people to tell us when we are wrong, to advise us on how to do right, and to encourage, support, arouse, cooperate, and inspire us along the way.
Author: David Brooks

Quote: Once firmly in place, the ball of cells undergoes the great divide—splitting into 2 groups. Half will become your son or daughter, while the other half will become the placenta, your baby’s lifeline during his or her uterine stay.
Author: Heidi Murkoff
Source: What to Expect When You're Expecting

Quote: Most important of all, however, is the fact that investing is fun. It’s fun to pit your intellect against that of the vast investment community and to find yourself rewarded with an increase in assets. It’s exciting to review your investment returns and to see how they are accumulating at a faster rate than your salary.
Author: Burton G. Malkiel
Source: A Random Walk Down Wall Street

Quote: What everyone should know is that it is very easy to tell a story about a stock. Your ability to tell a story has almost nothing to do with your ability to pick stocks. In the case of some successful managers, it is almost inversely correlated. Yet it is the staple, still, of many due diligence processes. By all means ask questions about stocks for entertainment and to illustrate the process of the manager. But don’t give it much weight in your due diligence.
Author: Paul Marshall

Quote: There is never one cause, never, there are infinite causes folding into infinite causes stretching through space and time in every direction forever and you will never find the beginning because there is no beginning, only threads touching threads touching threads into the dark.
Author: @lichthauch

Quote: Conversely, a business could be very philanthropic to its communities, but if it creates shoddy or harmful products, exploits its employees, cheats its suppliers, and does significant damage to the environment, it can hardly be considered an ethical or socially responsible business.
Author: John Mackey and Rajendra Sisodia (foreword by Bill George)

Quote: Bottom line: A supermajority of parents want every kid they’ve got. That’s part of the magic of having kids. Even parents of unplanned children often confess, “I can’t imagine my life without them.” The magic of not having kids, in contrast, is elusive. The childless can readily imagine being parents, and by the time they’re in their forties, most prefer this fantasy to their reality.
Author: Bryan Caplan
Source: Selfish Reasons to Have More Kids

Quote: Bruce Springsteen explained the choice presented to all parents: We are ghosts or we are ancestors in our children’s lives. We either lay our mistakes, our burdens upon them, and we haunt them, or we assist them in laying those old burdens down, and we free them from the chain of our own flawed behavior. And as ancestors, we walk alongside of them, and we assist them in finding their own way and some transcendence.
Author: Ryan Holiday

Quote: I advise all of you to read the annual reports of Berkshire Hathaway and Wesco Financial (Charles Munger is chairman). These reports are the best educational tools about how to think about investing and business. The lessons also show us how to behave in life.
Author: Peter Bevelin

Quote: You can maximize your child’s brain power in plenty of wonderful ways. After breast-feeding, focus on open-ended play, lots of verbal interaction, and praising effort—fertilizers statistically guaranteed to boost your child’s intellect from almost any starting point.
Author: John Medina

Quote: The most crucial adventure Suzanne took was her inner journey; the journey where she meditated for the first time and could sit still and listen to her true longings; the journey where she did some deep digging and learned she was meant to be doing something different, helping people one-on-one.
Author: Cady North

Quote: Nice Guys repress their feelings. Nice Guys tend to analyze rather than feel. They may see feelings as a waste of time and energy. They frequently try to keep their feelings on an even keel.
Author: Robert A. Glover

Quote: Looking back, I can’t think of anyone who regularly used drugs like aderall or modafinil in college and is now extraordinarily successful. They’re all managers / senior managers in consulting firms or VPs at banks.
Author: @cheafofstaff

Quote: A legacy family is not the act of building an ever higher house of cards. It's the process of laying a perpetually stronger foundation with each generation.
Author: @Dynastus
Theme: Purpose, Meaning & the Second Half of Life

Quote: Like-minded neighbors are providential, but there are advantages to conducting rhetoric-stage discussions by letter. You can file your letters and their answers as informal essays, and glance back through them to refresh your memory about the books you’ve read (and publish them once you become president).
Author: Susan Wise Bauer
Theme: Reading, Writing & the Life of the Mind

Quote: Yes, the more I thought about it, the more I realized that while I was busy trying to be hyperconscious, to learn how to be in the present moment, I’d already started doing it.
Author: "Eugene O'Kelly"

Quote: He that increaseth knowledge increaseth sorrow, and in much wisdom is much grief.
Author: Will Durant and Ariel Durant

Quote: I kept trying to picture my mother or somebody, or my aunt, or Sally Hayes's crazy mother, standing outside some department store and collecting dough for poor people in a beat-up old straw basket. It was hard to picture.
Author: J. D. Salinger

Quote: The Yerkes–Dodson law, named after the two psychologists who first described it at the beginning of the twentieth century, postulates that there is an inverse U relationship between stress and cognitive functioning.
Author: Dimitris Xygalatas

Quote: Here's the hard part: you have to be willing to lose status to leave the game. You have to be willing to say the dumb thing. Wear the unfashionable thing. Admit you don't know. Be uncool. Be unimpressive. Be invisble to the people who are still playing. And that feels like death at first. Because you've been using status as a proxy for worth for so long you forgot they're different things.
Author: @Kpaxs

Quote: Fertility crisis is immeasurably mysterious. Do you not want to witness immortality? Have you failed to imagine any serious future? Do you not imagine yourself creating ghosts? Do you not have desire in your hearts? Why do you shrink from the most natural of tradeoffs?
Author: @simonsarris
Theme: Reading, Writing & the Life of the Mind

Quote: My friend Todd Benson puts it this way: “Show up when it matters, when it means something. Never miss a funeral. Attend every wedding.”
Author: Scott Galloway

Quote: A second child always undermines parents’ belief in their power to mold their children, but child-rearing books hush this up because their market is first-time parents. —Steve Sailer, “The Nature of Nurture”
Author: Bryan Caplan
Source: Selfish Reasons to Have More Kids

Quote: For that reason, the meal has always been central to the Christian life and any exploration of the church must consider Christ’s table, its meaning, its place in our worship, and how it forms our life with God and one another.
Author: Skye Jethani
Source: What If Jesus Was Serious about the Church?

Quote: Indeed, the most vivid travel experiences usually find you by accident, and the qualities that will make you fall in love with a place are rarely the features that took you there.
Author: Rolf Potts

Quote: In social proof, it is not only action by others that misleads but also their inaction. In the presence of doubt, inaction by others becomes social proof that inaction is the right course. Thus, the inaction of a great many bystanders led to the death of Kitty Genovese in a famous incident much discussed in introductory psychology courses.
Author: Charles T. Munger (edited by Peter D. Kaufman)

Quote: When I was young, I wanted to be knowledgeable, but as I got older, I wanted to be wise. Wise people don’t just possess information; they possess a compassionate understanding of other people. They know about life.
Author: David Brooks

Quote: Beware, as Robert Woodson says, “In the process of giving our kids everything we didn’t have, we forget to give them what we did have.” Convince yourself of this simple and certain truth: For everything you give your child, you take something away.
Author: Richard Watts

Quote: While it has many upsides, a huge downside of taking a fast-paced job in a top tier company is the sheer lack of time during weekdays to get basic life tasks done: finances, home maintenance, various appointments. You try to catch up over weekends, but that further drains energy.
Author: @shreyas

Quote: Don’t count out Nick Train even if his consumer picks are extremely out of favour (to put it lightly). His U.K. wealth managers have also been clobbered. Recent addition of Universal Music in the global fund is notable. Having such a condensed portfolio well out of line with typical indexes is horrendous for career but you have to respect it. He owns his firm luckily or he would have been likely fired by now. Probably a good time to sharpen pensions on these names.
Author: @valuehurts

Quote: We “design” human nature, by designing the institutions within which people live. So we must ask ourselves just what kind of a human nature we want to help design.
Author: Barry Schwartz

Quote: Across the English Channel sociable use of the public domain is also high, as is the availability of gathering places. Each neighborhood, if not each block, has its cafe and, as in England, these have served to bring the residents into frequent and friendly contact with one another.
Author: Ray Oldenburg

Quote: Many of the benefits of children come later in life. If you are wisely selfish, you will not allow a few months of sleepwalking to stand between you and your future as a parent and grandparent.
Author: Bryan Caplan
Source: Selfish Reasons to Have More Kids

Quote: Strategies suggested to limit exposure included avoiding plastic food storage containers, fast foods, and high-fat foods (higher level of plasticizers that are fat soluble); using water bottles made of glass or steel and kitchen tools made of wood or steel; and limiting use of vinyl. Despite the urgent need to address the ever-growing plastic crisis, little has been done.
Author: Eric Topol

Quote: Harris Tweed is the only fabric that's legally protected. Just as Bordeaux wine has to be from the Bordeaux region of France, any fabric bearing this stamp has to be woven in the Outer Hebrides, finished in the Outer Hebrides, and made from wool dyed & spun in the Outer Hebrides.
Author: @dieworkwear

Quote: What possible role could optimism have played in our evolution as a species? Lionel Tiger argued in his book Optimism: The Biology of Hope (1979) that when early man left the forests and became hunters many of them suffered injury and death. Tiger suggests that humans tend to abandon tasks associated with negative consequences, so it was biologically adaptive for humans to develop a sense of optimism.
Author: James Montier
Source: The Little Book of Behavioral Investing

Quote: The second is that the jacket is entirely too small. Look at this suit by Anthony Sinclair, a legendary British tailor who created a pared-down style known as the "Conduit Cut" (called so bc Sinclair was located on Conduit Street when he tailored for Connery). Note clean lines.
Author: @dieworkwear

Quote: The pretense of knowledge is our most dangerous vice, because it prevents us from getting any better. Studious self-assessment is the antidote.
Author: Ryan Holiday

Quote: In theory, the risk of business failure can be reduced to a number, the probability of failure multiplied by the cost of failure. Sure, this turns out to be a subjective analysis, but in the process your own attitudes toward financial risk and reward are revealed.
Author: Randy Komisar with Kent Lineback

Quote: Suspend your own ego and do not expect reciprocity in the form of asking about you or what you do or about the things that you think make you great. Some people may actually care, but more often they are more interested in themselves. Let them share.
Author: Robin Dreeke

Quote: Money was safety. The pursuit of money, then, became a chase for safety and a flight from poverty, chaos, and the streets of my childhood.
Author: Jerry Colonna

Quote: A volume which catches everyone’s attention when, on my trips, I show it around is David Sucher’s City Comforts. Contained herein are many suggestions, all photographically-illustrated, as to “minor surgery” and modest additions which combine to make life out in the public domain more enticing, more comfortable, and more livable.
Author: Ray Oldenburg

Quote: His new schedule allowed him to leave home after his kids left for school. He works four days a week (two for Fidelity and two for charity), and Mondays he allocates entirely to his wife.
Author: Bob Buford

Quote: As scary as living can be, stop and think how you will feel if, on your deathbed, you look back on your life and conclude that you never really showed up because you were afraid. Isn’t that grim prospect more frightening than facing the fear itself, up front, now?
Author: James Hollis
Source: Finding Meaning in the Second Half of Life

Quote: In case you're unfamiliar, these are the levels. Participants were instructed that the moral units to allocate were like currency they can spend on others and allocate to different moral circles, and that a higher-level circle does **not** mean allocating to a lower one.
Author: @cremieuxrecueil

Quote: Pushing yourself to do hard things is cognitively taxing, but what's even more cognitively taxing is NOT pushing yourself and having to find ways to cope with your unrealized potential.
Author: @justinskycak

Quote: You’ve got to handle the first year of your business life about the way you would a trotting horse. Warm up a little before going to the post—not enough to be in a sweat, but just enough to be limber and eager. Never start off at a gait that you can’t improve on, but move along strong and well in hand to the quarter. Let out a notch there, but take it calm enough up to the half not to break, and hard enough not to fall back into the ruck.
Author: George Horace Lorimer
Source: Letters from a Self-Made Merchant to His Son

Quote: You must conform to rules, submit to a diet, refrain from dainties; exercise your body, whether you choose it or not, at a stated hour, in heat and cold; you must drink no cold water, and sometimes no wine—in a word, you must give yourself up to your trainer as to a physician.
Author: Epictetus

Quote: One of the best pieces of advice I’ve received for writing was a mantra: “Two crappy pages per day.”
Author: Tim Ferriss
Theme: Reading, Writing & the Life of the Mind

Quote: But the pursuit of knowledge is centered around a different ethic. The serious reader is not attempting to assimilate a huge quantity of information as quickly as possible, but to understand a few many-sided and elusive ideas. The speed ethic shouldn’t be transplanted into an endeavor that is governed by very different ideals.
Author: Susan Wise Bauer
Theme: Reading, Writing & the Life of the Mind

Quote: Remember that the chief characteristic of the modern era is that the responsibility for choosing values has shifted from the tribe and the institution to the individual. Such a privilege is also a responsibility.
Author: James Hollis
Source: Finding Meaning in the Second Half of Life
Theme: Purpose, Meaning & the Second Half of Life

Quote: Keeping vigil in the still, small hours, I remembered all the happy times we’d shared but also tortured myself with memories of times I had lost patience with him, spoken sharply or dismissively, and the one occasion when I had showed irritation and anger by slapping him in the face.
Author: Antonio Carluccio

Quote: Remember to remember: Help your kids exercise their memory by giving them lots of practice at recalling important events: in the car, at the dinner table, wherever.
Author: Daniel J. Siegel and Tina Payne Bryson

Quote: Write down the 3 to 5 things—and no more—that are making you the most anxious or uncomfortable. They’re often things that have been punted from one day’s to-do list to the next, to the next, to the next, and so on. Most important usually equals most uncomfortable, with some chance of rejection or conflict.
Author: Tim Ferriss
Theme: Reading, Writing & the Life of the Mind

Quote: Money has achieved such primacy in our lives that we often need to remind ourselves of the role it was intended to play — enabling ourselves (and those dear to us) to lead the life we have always imagined. As such, money is in service of a life well lived, not the other way around.
Author: Bogumil K. Baranowski

Quote: I'm a little annoyed that I spent so many years thinking keto was obviously good. Now that we have a lot of data, it turns out that the people who said "fat—especially saturated fat—clogs your arteries" were just totally correct. I should not have believed the contrarians.
Author: @cremieuxrecueil

Quote: He began by meeting with each of my key employees and asking them to talk, confidentially, about what went on around the firm. They apparently used expressions like "battered children," "mental abuse," "random violence," "rage disorder," and so on.
Author: Michael Steinhardt

Quote: Measure your wealth not by the things you can buy but by the things that no money can buy.
Author: Kevin Kelly

Quote: Consider Joe over there . . . ambitious, vain, self-absorbed. (Who then adds, “like me”?)
Author: James Hollis
Source: Why Good People Do Bad Things

Quote: More generally, I saw no need to ponder a philosophy of life. I instead felt comfortable with what is, for almost everyone, the default philosophy of life: to spend one’s days seeking an interesting mix of affluence, social status, and pleasure. My philosophy of life, in other words, was what might charitably be called an enlightened form of hedonism.
Author: William B. Irvine
Source: A Guide to the Good Life

Quote: Analytical inefficiencies may play an even greater role in driving outperformance in special situations. While information is generally available to investors willing to dig for it, many market participants struggle to overcome analytical hurdles.
Author: John Mihaljevic

Quote: THE CLEANSING POWER OF COLD “Often, exercise will make me feel better, meditating will make me feel better, but the ice bath is the greatest of all. It’s just magic—sauna, ice, back and forth. By the end of the fourth, or fifth, or sixth round of being in an ice tub, there is nothing in the world that bothers you.”
Author: Tim Ferriss

Quote: Each of them, in their times, was among the most powerful people on earth.
Author: Ward Farnsworth

Quote: The beauty of the soul shines out when a man bears with composure one heavy mischance after another, not because he does not feel them, but because he is a man of high and heroic temper. ~ Aristotle
Author: Sean DeLaney

Quote: Peter commanded us to be “prepared to make a defense to anyone who asks you for a reason for the hope that is in you” (1 Peter 3:15).
Author: Ron Rhodes

Quote: When child mortality declined and education (especially for women) and development increased, birth rates decreased. Humanity will need more innovation as absolute world population continues to grow, but the growth rate is declining, rapidly.
Author: David Epstein

Quote: Making knowledge productive is, in other words, a team game. The critical interdependencies in most complex high-value tasks mean that high-productivity people want to be matched with other high-productivity people.
Author: Lynda Gratton and Andrew J. Scott
Theme: Purpose, Meaning & the Second Half of Life

Quote: It was an interesting way to look at things. What would life be like if I was always doing things I wanted to do? What if I was always spending my time on something I was passionate about?
Author: John Strelecky
Source: The Cafe on the Edge of the World
Theme: Purpose, Meaning & the Second Half of Life

Quote: Although I was in a formal program, I didn’t want my curiosity to stop flowing. Inspired by the experimental mindset taught in scientific training, I asked myself: What experiment could I run on my own life that would bring me an intrinsic sense of fulfillment, whatever the outcome?
Author: Anne-Laure Le Cunff

Quote: While that anecdote might be true for the workforce, the rich and wealthy are doing the exact opposite. Many successful people are now leaving the UK and London, especially since the UK abolished the non-dom tax advantages. Instead they are opting to live in Milan (and also Dubai), since the Italian government started a non-dom tax advantaged golden visa some years back.
Author: @TihoBrkan

Quote: And when thus habituated, the phenomena of existence will not overwhelm you.
Author: Epictetus

Quote: I have conquered the need to conquer the world.
Author: Ryan Holiday and Stephen Hanselman

Quote: The waste of human and financial capital I mourn the opportunity costs (Anticipating the replies: opportunity costs seem unimportant until you realize that how much your salary can buy at the grocer, how good healthcare is, etc, all depend on low avg societal opportunity costs)
Author: @DellAnnaLuca

Quote: I don't have much interest in writing a book myself. Plus it would be a lot of work for somebody like me to try and do it in my seventies. And I have plenty else to do in life. So I'm not going to do it. But it's a screaming opportunity for somebody. And I'd provide funds to support the writing of an appropriate book if I found someone with the wisdom and the will to do the job right.
Author: Charles T. Munger (edited by Peter D. Kaufman)
Theme: Reading, Writing & the Life of the Mind

Quote: We overeat because there are signals and cues around us that tell us to eat. It’s simply not in our nature to pause after every bite and contemplate whether we’re full.
Author: Brian Wansink

Quote: Sure, you get it. You know that all things require work and that work might be quite difficult. But do you really understand? Do you have any idea just how much work there is going to be? Not work until you get your big break, not work until you make a name for yourself, but work, work, work, forever and ever.
Author: Ryan Holiday

Quote: Was the board furious at Kalanick for designing such an aggressive culture? On the contrary, they were thrilled as long as he was making them billions. They were only furious once he got caught—that is, once the flaws in the culture became widely known outside the company.
Author: Ben Horowitz
Source: What You Do Is Who You Are

Quote: Events come to us through lenses of judgment that are so familiar we forget we have them on. Stoics seek to become conscious of those judgments, to find the irrationality in them, and to choose them more carefully.
Author: Ward Farnsworth

Quote: It’s why people who struggle with binge eating disorders are taught to pause and think about how they’ll feel not while gorging on chocolate now but rather when they wake up the next morning.
Author: Brad Stulberg

Quote: Pro tip: Marry your best friend & person you can't fathom living without. Keep God in the center. Laugh with them. Grow with them. Raise awesome kids together. Resist becoming NORMAL as you age. Stay fit & healthy. Keep growing always. Society goes left, you go right. Society is for it, you are against it. Have a SMALL TRIBE & CIRCLE that's FOR YOU & you them. BE TERRIFIED OF BEING NORMAL.
Author: @tyromper

Quote: Prospective hindsight seems to spur more insights because it forces us to fill in the blanks between today and a certain future event (as opposed to the slipperier process of speculating about an event that may or may not happen).
Author: Chip Heath and Dan Heath

Quote: My dad's secretary told my sisters and me that one time the whole office was out having drinks and going around complaining about their wives and when it got time for my dad to say something he said idk I love my wife she's my best friend.
Author: @lillybilly299

Quote: Guest bios are a guaranteed part of my formula for giving you better attendance and a better party.
Author: Nick Gray

Quote: It’s your definition for what good work deserves to be. A Workview may address such questions as: Why work? What is work for? What does work mean? How does it relate to the individual, others, society? What defines good or worthwhile work? What does money have to do with it? What do experience, growth, and fulfillment have to do with it?
Author: Bill Burnett and Dave Evans

Quote: I suspect most people never spend down most of their retirement savings because to do so would be to acknowledge your impending death. I can't imagine how hard it would be to switch over psychologically from "saving for the future" to "the future is ending."
Author: @nateliason

Quote: Those with money to spare may also consider shoes especially made for lifting, such as Adidas Adipower, which is a personal favorite that greatly improves your squatting experience.
Author: Martin Berkhan
